paneContent:
I'll make the auto-advance only fire when the user actually submits in the current session, not when status comes
   from flow data.


в—Џ Locate edits (shell)
  в”‚ grep -n "handleSubmit\|useEffect\|useRef" src/prototypes/main.folder/StartupSignup/SignupForm.jsx
  в”” 5 lines...


в—Џ Edit SignupForm.jsx +1 -1
  в”” src/prototypes/main.folder/StartupSignup/SignupForm.jsx


в—Џ Edit SignupForm.jsx +4 -1
  в”” src/prototypes/main.folder/StartupSignup/SignupForm.jsx


в—Џ Save output (shell)
  в”‚ source .storyboard/terminals/sb-f3d5b3588da8.env
  в”‚ npx storyboard terminal output --summary "Gated submittingв†’success auto-advance behind a user-submit ref to
  в”‚ avoid hash pollution" --content "Fixed: auto-advance now only fires when handleSubmit set
  в”‚ userSubmittedRef.current=true. Visiting ?flow=submitting no longer writes signup.status=success into the URL
  в”‚ hash, so flow switches work again. NOTE: if user already has signup.status in their URL hash from earlier вЂ¦
  в”” 3 lines...


в—Џ Fixed. Auto-advance is now gated behind a userSubmittedRef flag set inside handleSubmit, so visiting
  ?flow=submitting no longer auto-writes signup.status=success into the hash. Flow switching should work again.

  Heads up: if your URL still has signup.status=... (or any signup.*=...) left in the hash from earlier testing,
  those overrides will continue to mask the flow's scene defaults. Clear the #... portion of the URL once, then
  click between the flow tiles вЂ” they should now drive Empty / Prefilled / Submitting / Success correctly.

 ~/workspace/storyboard-core/worktrees/0.6.0 [вЋ‡ 0.6.0*%]                                                @index.jsx
